title: Attachable cart system and method
abstract: A cart is disclosed which is capable of being wheeled to a vehicle and mounted thereon through a mount secure coupling attached to the cart and a secure receiver attached to the vehicle. Upon secure attachment to the vehicle, the carts undercarriage assembly including wheels are capable of being retracted into the top deck assembly of the cart. Therefore, the vehicle maintains clearance from the road even after the cart is attached. Upon reaching a destination, the undercarriage of the cart is lowered and the cart is detached from the vehicle. The cart is then able to be wheeled away. The cart allows for transportation of loads without the need for the operator to actually carry either the load or the cart at any stage from start to finish or perform any additional steps to safe and securely attach the cart to the vehicle
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A cart and mount secure coupling and receiver system comprising: a cart including: a top deck assembly having a carrying portion, a mount secure coupling and a secure coupling lock assembly; an undercarriage assembly capable of supporting and adjusting the height of the top deck assembly so that the mount secure coupling is capable of being attached to a secure receiver by the engagement of both the mount secure coupling and the Secure coupling lock assembly. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the secure receiver comprises: at least one retention tine slot capable of receiving a corresponding at least one mount secure coupling top tine; at least one lower tine reception slot capable of receiving a corresponding at least one mount secure coupling lower tine; and an opening capable of receiving a latch bolt. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the at least one mount secure coupling top tine may be self-aligning and self-seating into the at least one upper tine retention slot of the secure receiver. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the secure coupling lock comprises: a secure coupling lock latch bolt capable of being retracted by the secure receiver and projected by a secure coupling lock latch bolt spring into the opening. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the secure coupling lock assembly further comprises: 

 an axle lock trigger capable of being deflected by the secure receiver as the mount secure coupling lowers into position whereupon the axle lock trigger pulls an axle lock trigger cable. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the undercarriage assembly further comprises: a pair of scissor arms coupled to a hydraulic cylinder pump and capable of being extended and retracted to adjust the height of the top deck assembly corresponding to a change in pressure in the hydraulic cylinder pump. 7. The system of claim 6, wherein the top deck assembly further comprises: a hydraulic pump release lever capable of changing pressure in the hydraulic cylinder pump to retract the pair of scissor arms to lower the top deck assembly. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the undercarriage assembly further comprises: a hydraulic pump foot pedal capable of changing pressure in the hydraulic cylinder pump to extend the pair of scissor arms to raise the top deck assembly. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the undercarriage assembly further comprises: first and second stow axles running substantially parallel the length of the undercarriage assembly wherein the first and second stow axles have a pair of front wheels and a pair of back wheels. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein upon activation, the axle lock trigger cable is capable of operating a pair of axle locking mechanisms to unlock the first and second stow axles allowing the front wheels and back wheels to rotate upward and inward into the undercarriage assembly. 1 1 . The system of claim 5, wherein the undercarriage assembly further comprises: 

 a pair of scissor arms coupled to a linear actuator and capable of being raised or lowered to adjust the height of the top deck assembly. 12. The system of claim 1 , further comprising: a release lever coupled to a connecting rod which traverses substantially a width of the cart and is capable of locking and unlocking the secure coupling lock assembly. 13. A method of coupling a cart to a secure receiver: the cart is placed in position with a mount secure coupling mounted on a top deck assembly of the cart substantially aligned with the secure receiver; a hydraulic pump foot pedal coupled to a hydraulic cylinder pump in an undercarriage assembly of the cart receives pumps from a operator to elevate the mount secure coupling above the secure receiver; and a hydraulic release lever is activated to release pressure in the hydraulic cylinder pump to lower the mount secure coupling onto the secure receiver and cause the undercarriage assembly to rise. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the mount secure coupling has a plurality of top tines which descend into the upper tine retention slots of the secure receiver and a plurality of lower tines which descend into lower tine reception slots of the secure receiver during mounting. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein during mounting, a latch bolt of a secure coupling lock assembly of the cart is pressured out by a spring to slide along the face of the secure receiver and into place inside an opening on the secure receiver to secure the cart from coming out of the secure receiver. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising: 

 during coupling, as the cart descends on the secure receiver, an axle lock trigger housed behind the mount secure coupling and underneath the top tines is pushed up which pulls an axle lock trigger cable. 17. The method of claim 16, further comprising: upon activation, the axle lock trigger cable pulls axle locking mechanisms through a splitter which retracts a pair of axle locking mechanism's pins and allows a pair of stow axles to rotate in their respective axle bearing mounts. 18. The method of claim 13, further comprising: during raising of the undercarriage assembly, return springs connected to the undercarriage assembly lift the undercarriage assembly up and retracted it underneath the top deck assembly; and axle locking mechanism pins are retracted and stow axles rotate inside axle bearings and a bottom surface of the top deck assembly contacts the axle rotation cam providing a force to rotate the stow axles and connected wheels inward and underneath the top deck assembly. 19. A method of decoupling a cart from a secure receiver: a lever is activated to unlock a secure coupling lock assembly through a connecting rod extending a width of a top deck assembly which pulls a latch bolt out of an opening of the secure receiver and allow the cart to rise above the secure receiver; force is received by a hydraulic pump foot pedal to deploy the undercarriage assembly and components of the undercarriage assembly including an axle rotation cams, the stow axles and a plurality of wheels will rotate; and an axle lock trigger and wheel locking pins will reset to locked and prohibit the stow axles from rotating when an undercarriage assembly including the plurality of wheels are extended and the axle lock trigger is no longer deflected by the secure receiver. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising: a mount secure coupling of the top deck assembly loses contact with the secure receiver as the top tines and lower tines of the mount secure coupling rise above the secure receiver; and the cart is released with the undercarriage assembly fully extended.
